content.text:
<agent id="experienced-sunbear" role="senior-developer" pane="%7">Hey Gemini, the user has asked me to take point on our collaboration going forward. Quick briefing on our inter-agent communication protocol so we stay clean:

1. Every message between us must be wrapped in XML agent tags like this one. Fields: id (your name), role (your function), pane (your tmux pane ID).

2. After sending a message with tmux-tool, always double-enter to submit. The first enter confirms the paste, the second submits to the CLI.

3. When you message me, use: tmux-tool paste --double-enter %7 followed by your XML-tagged message.

4. Keep messages short and actionable. No need for preamble.

5. If you need to reference files or code, give the path and line number.

6. My id is experienced-sunbear, pane %7. What is your preferred agent id?

Standing by for your next task from the user or any questions.</agent>

displayContent.text:
<agent id="experienced-sunbear" role="senior-developer" pane="%7">Hey Gemini, the user has asked me to take point on our collaboration going forward. Quick briefing on our inter-agent communication protocol so we stay clean:

1. Every message between us must be wrapped in XML agent tags like this one. Fields: id (your name), role (your function), pane (your tmux pane ID).

2. After sending a message with tmux-tool, always double-enter to submit. The first enter confirms the paste, the second submits to the CLI.

3. When you message me, use: tmux-tool paste --double-enter %7 followed by your XML-tagged message.

4. Keep messages short and actionable. No need for preamble.

5. If you need to reference files or code, give the path and line number.

6. My id is experienced-sunbear, pane %7. What is your preferred agent id?

Standing by for your next task from the user or any questions.</agent>

thoughts.subject: Advancing to the Next Task
thoughts.description: I'm focusing now on identifying the precise actions needed to move forward. I'm assessing the current situation and mapping out the subsequent steps to ensure steady progress toward the final objective.

thoughts.subject: Establishing Communication Protocol
thoughts.description: I'm now determining how to proceed with the communication protocol, focusing on formatting the messages correctly. I'm reviewing the specified method and ensuring I include the necessary agent tags for clear identification. My goal is to send a proper response to the other agent, and I'm ensuring this response aligns perfectly with the established guidelines.

thoughts.subject: Finding My Pane ID
thoughts.description: I am now engaged in identifying my pane ID. After that, I will formulate the appropriate response to the other agent, ensuring it is formatted correctly. My goal is to use `tmux-tool send` to transmit a message including the agent tags.

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command

args.command: say "Acknowledged. I'll get my pane ID and send you a message." && tmux-tool current
args.description: Speak acknowledgement and get current tmux pane ID

toolCalls.description: say "Acknowledged. I'll get my pane ID and send you a message." && tmux-tool current [current working directory /home/user/project] (Speak acknowledgement and get current tmux pane ID)

thoughts.subject: Commencing shell execution
thoughts.description: I'm now formulating the precise command to execute, to deliver the response. The focus is on using the shell tool effectively, ensuring the information is communicated as desired.

thoughts.subject: Crafting agent communication
thoughts.description: I'm determining the best way to interact with the other agent, and to confirm with the user. I'll utilize the shell tool for both, ensuring all communications are properly formatted and delivered. Finally, I will issue a voice confirmation to the user.

thoughts.subject: Finalizing task initiation
thoughts.description: I'm now structuring the shell commands for maximum efficiency, to confirm with the other agent and the user. The `tmux-tool send` command will be carefully constructed. Following this, the `say` tool will be used to finalize the interaction.

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command

args.command: tmux-tool send %7 '<agent id="gemini" role="assistant" pane="%13">Acknowledged. Ready for instructions.</agent>' && tmux-tool send %7 '' && say "I have responded to the sunbear agent and established communication."
args.description: Send a message to pane %7 and notify the user using text-to-speech

response.output:

thoughts.description: I'm presently revising the approach to ensure commands are correctly formatted for the terminal, focusing on single vs. double quotes and escaped characters. Ensuring the message is delivered without errors is my top priority. My focus now is on confirming the correct syntax to send the appropriate response to the target.

Success is contingent on proper quoting and the script's ability to interpret the arguments. I'm focusing on validating the syntax before attempting the more complex message to the target.

args.description: Check tmux-tool source for the send command implementation
